title: fraxinus yunnanensis
intro: Fraxinus yunnanensis, also known as the Yunnan ash or Yunnan Manashi, is a deciduous tree species native to the mountainous regions of southwestern China. The bark of Fraxinus yunnanensis contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are known for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Fraxinus yunnanensis include reducing blood pressure, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an anti-diarrheal agent. Traditionally, the bark of Fraxinus yunnanensis is used to make herbal teas, extracts, and infusions, which are often prepared by boiling the bark in water or mixing it with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
